lessons.title: Accounting Concepts and Principles
objectives: Explain core concepts like Business Entity and Accruals
concepts: Accounting is the process of recording, summarizing, and communicating financial information.
Business Entity: The business is separate from its owner.

experiment.description: Concept Match: Match accounting principles (e.g., Going Concern, Prudence) with their correct definitions.

lessons.title: The Accounting Equation
objectives: Apply the accounting equation: Assets = Liabilities + Capital
concepts: Assets are resources owned by the business.
Liabilities are debts owed to outsiders.

experiment.description: Equation Balance: Drag items like 'Cash', 'Loan', and 'Furniture' to the correct side of a balance scale to keep the accounting equation balanced.

lessons.title: Double Entry Bookkeeping
objectives: Record transactions in T-accounts using debit and credit rules
concepts: Every transaction affects at least two accounts.
Debit (Dr) on the left; Credit (Cr) on the right.
Increase in Assets -> Debit; Increase in Liabilities -> Credit.

experiment.description: T-Account Tracker: Enter transactions (e.g., 'Paid rent by cash $500') and click the correct side (Dr/Cr) of the relevant accounts to see the system balance.

lessons.title: Journals and Daybooks
objectives: Record transactions in the Sales, Purchases, and General Journals
concepts: Daybooks are used to record transactions before they are posted to the ledger.

experiment.description: Journal Entry: Fill out a digital sales journal based on a set of provided invoices.

lessons.title: The Cash Book
objectives: Prepare a two-column and three-column Cash Book
concepts: A Cash Book acts as both a book of original entry and a ledger account.
Contra entries involve both cash and bank.

experiment.description: Cash Book Builder: Enter cash and bank transactions into a template and calculate the closing balances.

lessons.title: Preparing a Trial Balance
objectives: List account balances to verify double entry accuracy
concepts: A Trial Balance is a list of all ledger balances at a point in time.
Total Debits should equal Total Credits.

experiment.description: Balance Checker: Drag ledger balances into a Trial Balance template and see if they balance. If not, troubleshoot the errors.

lessons.title: The Income Statement
objectives: Prepare a Trading and Profit & Loss Account
concepts: Calculates Gross Profit and Net Profit.
Sales - Cost of Goods Sold = Gross Profit.

experiment.description: Profit Calculator: Drag income and expense items into the correct sections of an Income Statement to calculate the final net profit.

lessons.title: Statement of Financial Position (Balance Sheet)
objectives: Classify assets and liabilities and prepare a Balance Sheet
concepts: Shows the financial position of a business at a specific date.

experiment.description: Asset/Liability Sort: Drag various items into buckets (Current Asset, Non-current Liability, etc.) to build a complete Balance Sheet.

lessons.title: Accruals and Prepayments
objectives: Adjust accounts for expenses and income not yet paid or received
concepts: Accrued Expenses: Expenses incurred but not yet paid (Liability).
Prepaid Expenses: Expenses paid in advance (Asset).

experiment.description: Time Adjustment: Use a timeline slider to see how paying an expense early (prepayment) or late (accrual) affects the current year's profit.
